course_description.overview: A loop-based ultra-distance cycling event starting and finishing in Conwy, North Wales, that crosses into Ireland and explores the heartlands of the Celtic Nations. The event emphasizes journeys of discovery through six modern Celtic nations with deep cultural and historical narratives.

key_segments.name: Conwy Loop
key_segments.description: Primary route starting and finishing in the historic town of Conwy, North Wales, crossing into Ireland and exploring Celtic territories

course_description.difficulty_narrative: An extreme ultra-distance event requiring exceptional endurance, with significant elevation gain (25,795 m) spread across approximately 1,427 miles of cycling through varied terrain including road and gravel surfaces across multiple Celtic nations.

logistics.getting_there: Event based in Conwy, North Wales. Accessible via major UK transport networks to Wales.
logistics.accommodation: Not specified in available sources. Riders should plan accommodation in Conwy or surrounding areas.
logistics.registration_details: Registration information available at pancelticrace.com/ultra/2026. Sign-on and rider briefing details not fully specified in search results.
logistics.bike_logistics: Not specified. Multi-day ultra event likely requires support crew or self-supported bikepacking setup.
logistics.local_considerations: Event traverses historic Celtic territories with cultural significance. Route crosses from Wales into Ireland, requiring awareness of border crossings and local conditions.

final_verdict.one_liner: An epic, culturally-immersive ultra-distance cycling odyssey through Celtic nations for experienced riders seeking adventure over competition.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-distance cyclists with exceptional endurance capacity, cultural curiosity, and self-sufficiency skills. Suited for riders comfortable with multi-day bikepacking or supported touring through remote Celtic territories.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the right rider. The event offers notable cultural immersion and adventure through historic Celtic landscapes, though logistics complexity and limited race infrastructure require careful planning.
pro_tips: Start training 6+ months in advance for the extreme distance and elevation
Research Celtic history and culture along the route to maximize the experience
Plan support crew logistics thoroughly or prepare for self-supported bikepacking
Visit pancelticrace.com/ultra/2026 for official route details and registration
